rules.label: Rules
rules.value:
🏁 PMR MX-5 League: Official Rules & Regulations 🏁

Point Scoring
1st place        10
2nd place        7
3rd place        5
4th place        3
5th place        2
6th place        1
Fastest lap bonus        2

The primary focus of this league is developing strong, clean racecraft.
Because MX-5 racing relies heavily on momentum, smooth, predictable, and respectful driving will always yield the best results.

While we are all friends — we race hard. These rules ensure competition remains fair, consistent, and enjoyable for everyone.

1. Overtaking, Defending & Racing Rights
The Burden of Passing

The responsibility for completing a pass safely always lies with the overtaking driver. Moves must be controlled and realistic — no divebombs or reckless lunges.

The Racing Line is Not Protected

Drivers do not own the racing line.
If a car has legitimately established overlap, you must adjust your line to avoid contact.

Earning the Corner (Overlap Rule)

An attacking driver earns the right to space at the corner if:

Their front wheels are at least level with the defending driver’s cockpit
This overlap is established before the turn-in point

👉 If these conditions are not met, the corner belongs to the defending driver.

Maintaining Overlap

Overlap must be sustained through the corner.
If the attacking car drops back, they lose the right to space.

Leaving Racing Room

When overlap is established:

Both drivers must leave at least one car’s width of space
This applies at corner entry, apex, and exit

🚫 Squeezing, drifting wide on exit, or forcing a car off track is not allowed.

2. Defensive Driving & Conduct
One Move Rule

Drivers may make one defensive move on a straight.

🚫 Weaving, reactive blocking, or making multiple moves is not allowed.

No Moving Under Braking

Once braking has begun, drivers must hold their line.

🚫 Changing direction under braking is strictly prohibited and a major cause of incidents.

Predictability

Defending drivers must be consistent and predictable in their movements.
Erratic driving will be penalised.

Contact & Control

Minor contact can happen in close racing, but:

Careless or avoidable contact is not acceptable
Repeated or aggressive contact will be penalised
Safe Re-Entry

If you leave the track:

You have no right to the racing line
You must rejoin only when safe

🚫 Rejoining into traffic and causing an incident is a serious offense

Redress (Giving Positions Back)

If you gain an advantage through contact:

You are expected to give the position back immediately

✅ This will usually resolve the incident without further action

3. Post-Race Procedures & Stewarding

To keep the league clean and drama-free, all incidents are handled through a formal process:

Save Replay (Mandatory)

All drivers must save their race replay after each event.

Cool-Down Rule

🚫 No arguing or debating incidents in race chat or lobby

Keep it respectful — incidents are reviewed after the race.

How to Report an Incident

If no redress is given:

Record the incident from the replay (both cars if possible)
Upload to YouTube (Unlisted is fine)
Send the link to the League Admin within 24 hours
Steward Decisions
Incidents will be reviewed by the Stewards
Decisions are final

Penalties may include:

Time penalties
Position drops
Warnings or race bans (for repeated offences)
4. Official Lobby Settings
Damage: ON
In-Game Penalties: ON
Assists: AUTHENTIC
Track Temperature: AVERAGE

These settings are designed to promote close, fair, and realistic racing.

🏁 Final Note

Race hard. Race fair. Respect each other.

Good racecraft creates better racing for everyone — and that’s what this league is all about.
